text.title: Go Project Layout: internal/, cmd/, pkg/, Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Standard directory layout with internal/ as default, pkg/ only for public API, cmd/ for entry points, and the three most common layout anti-patterns.

section.title: Directory Layout

rule.statement: Use internal/ as the default for all application code; add pkg/ only when an external module explicitly imports it.
rule.rationale: Go's toolchain enforces internal/ boundaries — external packages cannot import it. pkg/ without a clear "external consumers" rule becomes a dumping ground.

section.p: Standard tree: cmd/api/main.go + cmd/worker/main.go. internal/config/, internal/database/, internal/handler/, internal/middleware/, internal/model/, internal/repository/, internal/service/. pkg/ (public library only). api/ (OpenAPI spec). scripts/, deployments/ (Dockerfile, k8s). go.mod, go.sum, Makefile.

section.title: Module Initialization

example.code: go mod init github.com/username/project go get github.com/gin-gonic/gin@latest go mod tidy go mod verify

section.reference: Bash script to materialise standard tree from module path + binary names

antipattern.description: Cyclic imports: package A imports B, package B imports A.
antipattern.reason: Go toolchain rejects cyclic imports at compile time. The fix is to extract shared types into a third package (e.g., internal/model), not to export more symbols upward.

antipattern.description: Global state: var DB *sql.DB at package level.
antipattern.reason: Global DB makes parallel tests interfere, prevents multiple instances, and hides dependencies. Use constructor injection: NewUserRepository(db *sql.DB).

antipattern.description: Everything in the main package: type User, type Order, func CreateUser, func HandleRequest all in package main.
antipattern.reason: Single-package projects become untestable and unmaintainable above ~500 lines. Separate by responsibility into internal/ sub-packages.
